summary: Large HSD JObj decompilation pass that removed asm/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.s from the build, added most of its implementation to src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.c, expanded include/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h, and propagated newly identified JObj/Mtx symbol names into dependent assembly and C callsites. The PR body says all but 3 JObj functions were matched, with the remaining functions kept as NON_MATCHING but functionally equivalent due to register-swap or stack-usage issues. It also fixed shiftability. Review feedback focused on follow-up cleanup: flag-test macros, TODO tracking for USE_QUATERNION, and naming based on assert strings such as envelope_mtx and union_type_dobj.
what_changed: Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.s, removing 4881 lines of standalone JObj assembly.
Added a large C implementation in src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.c, with 1817 added lines and only small edits to existing code.
Expanded include/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h with JObj declarations, flags, and supporting types/macros needed by the C implementation.
Updated obj_files.mk to stop building the old JObj assembly object.

Renamed multiple callsites from anonymous func_8037xxxx-style labels to identified baselib symbols, including HSD_JObjSetDPtclCallback, HSD_JObjWalkTree, HSD_JObjAddDObj, HSD_MtxGetRotation, and HSD_MtxGetScale.
Left the last unmatched JObj routines under NON_MATCHING with inline assembly fallbacks, with comments linking decomp.me scratches for HSD_JObjAddAnim and HSD_JObjAddAnimAll.
The PR body explicitly notes that all but 3 JObj functions matched and that remaining NON_MATCHING code should be functionally equivalent despite register-swap or stack-usage issues.
The PR body also notes that the pass fixed shiftability, though the provided slice does not show the precise shiftability failure mechanism.
smart_moves: Converted the module wholesale enough to remove the old assembly file while keeping inline asm fallbacks for the few functions that were not yet byte-matching.
Propagated newly identified symbol names into unrelated assembly/C callsites, making future cross-module work more searchable and less dependent on raw func_ addresses.
Documented nonmatching functions with decomp.me scratch links and explanatory comments, preserving the current matching state for later cleanup.
Kept functionally equivalent C for hard cases while guarding it with NON_MATCHING, allowing reviewers and future decompilers to reason about behavior without sacrificing matching builds.
Identified recurring JObj flag-test patterns, creating an opportunity for a shared macro rather than repeated ternary idioms.
decomp_lessons: When moving a large baselib assembly module to C, inline asm fallbacks can bridge the final few nonmatching functions while still allowing the main asm file to be deleted.
If a function is functionally understood but blocked by register swaps or stack usage, keep the readable C under NON_MATCHING and preserve the matching assembly path.
After naming a core baselib routine, update all known callers immediately; this improves future reverse engineering in game modules and neighboring baselib files.
Repeated flag checks such as `jobj->flags & 0x4020 ? FALSE : TRUE` should likely become macros once the flag semantics are settled.
Assert strings can be important evidence for field or macro names; reviewers suggested names like `envelope_mtx` and noted likely cleanup around `union_type_dobj`.
Do not treat provisional flags/macros as final if comments say follow-up cleanup is expected; USE_QUATERNION was explicitly marked `TODO remove` and reviewers suggested filing an issue.
naming_conventions: Prefer HSD-prefixed baselib names for identified JObj and Mtx routines: HSD_JObjSetDPtclCallback, HSD_JObjWalkTree, HSD_JObjAddDObj, HSD_MtxGetRotation, HSD_MtxGetScale.
Use JObj/DObj/RObj/AObj capitalization in HSD symbol names, matching existing baselib style.
Reviewer feedback suggests field or local names should follow assert-symbol evidence when available; otherwise use descriptive snake_case such as `envelope_mtx`.
Review discussion mentions `union_type_dobj` as probably the actual definition name for a macro/union concept, but also says many macros still needed an assert-string cleanup pass.
Flag constants in jobj.h are uppercase bit defines such as SPLINE, FLIP_IK, SPECULAR, USE_QUATERNION, CLASSICAL_SCALE, and INSTANCE; some names may still be provisional.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted an entire module-level assembly file after moving most implementation to C.
Used `#ifdef NON_MATCHING` C implementations paired with `#else` inline `asm void` versions for functions that were understood but not yet matching.
Retained exact original assembly in inline asm fallbacks for HSD_JObjAddAnim and HSD_JObjAddAnimAll in order to preserve matching output.
Used comments to record why functions remained nonmatching: register swaps and stack usage.
Updated assembly comments/callsites to call newly named functions without changing instruction bytes, improving symbol visibility while preserving binary equivalence.
Kept decomp.me scratch links near unresolved functions so future work can resume from known experiments.

review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer noted that the `USE_QUATERNION` TODO removal should become an issue.

Reviewer observed that repeated flag-test ternaries like `x & flags ? FALSE : TRUE` appear often and should likely be wrapped in a macro, though it was unclear whether the positive and negative forms can share one macro.
Reviewer asked whether a name was an assert symbol; if not, it should be `envelope_mtx`.
Reviewer/comment discussion said `union_type_dobj` is probably the actual definition, and that many other macros need to be changed based on assert strings in a later cleanup pass.

follow_up_queries: Find the 3 remaining NON_MATCHING functions in src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.c after PR 535.
Search for repeated `flags ? FALSE : TRUE` or `flags ? TRUE : FALSE` JObj flag-test patterns that should become macros.
Track whether an issue was created to remove or resolve USE_QUATERNION.
Search assert strings for envelope_mtx and union_type_dobj to validate names and related macros.
Inspect later PRs touching HSD_JObjAddAnim and HSD_JObjAddAnimAll to see how the register-swap or stack-usage issues were resolved.
Check later shiftability fixes around sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.c if the exact mechanism is needed.
